summary.narrative: Mayor Keller has made progress on renewable energy commitments and youth programs, though his flagship promise of 100% renewable energy by 2025 was not fully achieved by the deadline. His administration continues work on public safety and economic development initiatives, with mixed results on crime reduction goals.

promises.text: Switch the City of Albuquerque to 100% renewables by 2025
promises.evidence: While significant progress was made on renewable energy adoption, the city did not achieve 100% renewable energy by the 2025 deadline as promised during the campaign.

promises.text: Expand youth programs and services across Albuquerque
promises.evidence: The Keller administration expanded youth programs including job training, recreation, and after-school services, which was highlighted as an accomplishment in his re-election campaign.

promises.text: Take bold action on climate and environmental sustainability
promises.evidence: The administration has implemented various climate action initiatives and continues to pursue sustainability goals, though the most ambitious renewable energy target was missed.

promises.text: Reduce violent crime and improve public safety in Albuquerque
promises.evidence: Crime reduction has been a central focus with increased police hiring and community programs, though Albuquerque continues to face significant public safety challenges. Keller defeated a law-and-order challenger in 2024.

promises.text: Strengthen the local economy and create jobs
promises.evidence: Economic development initiatives have been implemented including business incentives and workforce programs, with the administration citing economic progress in the 2025 State of the City address.

promises.text: Increase transparency and accountability in city government
promises.evidence: The administration has published regular progress reports and accomplishment summaries, and maintained public communication about city initiatives.

promises.text: Address homelessness through comprehensive services and housing
promises.evidence: While some initiatives have been launched, homelessness remains a persistent challenge in Albuquerque with limited measurable progress toward stated goals.
